message.content:
import { Map, Swords, Trophy, Target, Shield, Sparkles } from 'lucide-react'
import { Callout } from '@/components/mdx/Callout'
import { Card, CardGrid } from '@/components/mdx/CardGrid'
import { Checklist } from '@/components/mdx/Checklist'
import { FAQ } from '@/components/mdx/FAQ'
import { Steps, Step } from '@/components/mdx/Steps'
import { Section } from '@/components/mdx/Section'
import { YouTubeEmbed } from '@/components/mdx/YouTubeEmbed'

export const metadata = {
  title: "Anime Tactical Simulator Wiki: Complete Strategy & Codes Guide 2026",
  description: "Master Anime Tactical Simulator with our comprehensive wiki. Find the latest codes, unit tier lists, evolution guides, and tower strategies for 2026.",
  category: "Guide",
  image: "https://placehold.co/800x400/1e293b/fff?text=Anime+Tactical+Simulator+Wiki",
  date: "2026-03-04",
  author: "Anime Wiki Team"
}

<Callout type="info" title="Quick Guide">
- **Anime Tactical Simulator Wiki**: Your ultimate resource for mastering unit placement and squad building.
- **Active Codes**: Use **RELEASE**, **UPDATE1**, and **1MVISITLETSGOO** for massive early-game boosts.
- **Unit Management**: Prioritize **Secret** and **Mythic** units; fuse lower rarities for experience.
- **Tower Mode**: Essential for unlocking **extra unit slots** to maximize your team's damage.
- **Raids**: Farm **Stat Cubes** and **Medals** once you have a stable team of at least 3-4 Mythics.
</Callout>

Anime Tactical Simulator has taken the Roblox world by storm in 2026, offering a deep, strategic tower defense experience that challenges even the most seasoned players. As you dive into this vibrant universe, utilizing an **anime tactical simulator wiki** becomes essential for optimizing your progression and staying ahead of the power creep. Whether you are summoning your favorite heroes from iconic series or meticulously planning your defense against waves of challenging enemies, understanding the underlying mechanics is the difference between victory and defeat.

This guide serves as your definitive **anime tactical simulator wiki**, providing everything from the latest redeemable codes to advanced raid tactics. We have analyzed the current meta to bring you the most efficient ways to farm Yen, upgrade your talents, and evolve your units into their final, most powerful forms. By following this roadmap, you will transform from a novice commander into a tactical mastermind capable of conquering the highest floors of the Tower and dominating the most difficult raids.

<Section icon={Map} title="Active Codes and Redemption Guide">

The developers at Tsukikage frequently release promotional codes to celebrate milestones and updates. These codes are the fastest way to acquire Gems, Dungeon Keys, and Potions without spending hours grinding. For any player looking to climb the leaderboards, staying updated with the latest entries in the **anime tactical simulator wiki** code list is a top priority.

| Code | Rewards | Requirement |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **RELEASE** | 500 Gems, 5 Dungeon Keys | None |
| **UPDATE1** | 1000 Gems, 10 Potions | None |
| **1MVISITLETSGOO** | 250 Gems, 3 Wheels, 2 Keys | New Milestone |
| **THANKYOU** | 5 Dungeon Keys, 500 Yen | None |
| **DISCORD** | 3 Talent Rerolls, 2 Premium Rerolls | Join Group |
| **MILESTONE** | 750 Gems, 5 Potions | None |
| **LAUNCH** | 1000 Gems, 10 Dungeon Keys | None |

<Callout type="success" title="Redemption Tip">
Codes in Anime Tactical Simulator are case-sensitive. To ensure you receive your rewards, we recommend copying and pasting them directly from this list. Many codes, like **DISCORD**, require you to be a member of the official Roblox group before they can be activated.
</Callout>

<Steps>
  <Step num="1" title="Launch the Experience">
    Open Anime Tactical Simulator on the Roblox platform and allow all assets to load completely to avoid UI glitches.
  </Step>
  <Step num="2" title="Access the Menu">
    Locate the **Codes** button on the left side of your screen, which is typically represented by a gift icon.
  </Step>
  <Step num="3" title="Input the Code">
    Enter the active code into the text field. Ensure there are no leading or trailing spaces.
  </Step>
  <Step num="4" title="Claim Rewards">
    Click the **Redeem** button. Your inventory will be updated instantly with Gems, Keys, or reroll items.
  </Step>
</Steps>

</Section>

<Section icon={Target} title="Beginner Progression & World Strategy">

Navigating the various worlds in Anime Tactical Simulator requires a balance of resource management and tactical patience. Each world introduces a significant jump in enemy health (HP) and difficulty, making it vital to prepare your squad before moving forward. The primary goal in the early game is to accumulate enough Yen to unlock new areas while simultaneously strengthening your core units.

<CardGrid cols={3}>
  <Card title="World 1: Starter">
    - **Focus**: Complete basic kill quests.
    - **Goal**: Farm Yen for World 2.
    - **Strategy**: Skip heavy boss grinds early.
  </Card>
  <Card title="World 2: Growth">
    - **Focus**: Unlock stat upgrades.
    - **Goal**: Build a full Mythic team.
    - **Strategy**: Start farming early raids.
  </Card>
  <Card title="World 3+: Advanced">
    - **Focus**: Handle massive HP jumps.
    - **Goal**: Evolve core units.
    - **Strategy**: Optimize traits and talents.
  </Card>
</CardGrid>

<Callout type="warning" title="Progression Warning">
Do not rush into World 3 until you have at least 3-4 Mythic units at Level 50+. The enemy HP increase is substantial, and a weak team will find themselves unable to clear stages efficiently, leading to wasted time.
</Callout>

| World | Unlock Requirement | Priority Activity |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **World 1** | Default Access | Quest Completion, Code Redemption |
| **World 2** | Yen Payment | Stat Upgrades, Summoning Mythics |
| **World 3** | High Yen Cost | Trait Rerolling, Unit Evolution |
| **World 4** | Maximum Yen | End-game Raids, Talent Maxing |

</Section>

<Section icon={Swords} title="Unit Management: Keep, Fuse, or Sell">

Effective unit management is the backbone of any successful strategy in Anime Tactical Simulator. Your inventory will quickly fill with units of varying rarities, from Common to Secret. Knowing which ones to invest in and which ones to discard is crucial for maintaining a powerful roster and securing the materials needed for high-level evolution.

**Unit Rarity Strategy:**
- **Keep**: Always retain Secret, Mythic, and Legendary units. These are your primary damage dealers and will be required for late-game content and evolution.
- **Fuse**: Use Common, Uncommon, and Rare units as "fodder" to level up your higher-tier units. This is the most efficient way to boost your team's power early on.
- **Sell**: Selling units provides **Souls**, which are a mandatory resource for evolution. Focus on selling excess high-rarity units once you have established your main squad.

| Action | Rarity Level | Resulting Benefit |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Keep** | Secret / Mythic | Long-term power and evolution potential |
| **Fuse** | Common / Rare | Fast EXP gain for your main squad units |
| **Sell** | Epic / Legendary | Souls required for the evolution process |

<Callout type="tip" title="Evolution Insight">
Evolution is not just about power; it is about utility. Some units gain entirely new abilities or increased range upon evolving. Check the **anime tactical simulator wiki** evolution requirements frequently to see which duplicates you need to save.
</Callout>

<Checklist
  id="unit-goals"
  title="Unit Management Checklist:"
  items={[
    "Lock all Mythic and Secret units immediately",
    "Fuse all Common units into your primary DPS",
    "Save duplicate Mythics for late-game evolution",
    "Sell excess units only when Souls are needed",
    "Prioritize leveling units with high base damage"
  ]}
/>

</Section>

<Section icon={Trophy} title="Tower Mode and Raid Tactics">

Tower Mode and Raids represent the competitive and cooperative peaks of Anime Tactical Simulator. While the story mode provides the foundation, these modes offer the permanent upgrades and rare materials necessary for true mastery. Tower Mode, in particular, should be your secondary focus as soon as your team is stable, as it unlocks the most valuable resource in the game: **Unit Slots**.

**Tower Milestone Rewards:**
- **Floor 10**: +1 Unit Slot (Critical)
- **Floor 25**: +1 Unit Slot (Critical)
- **Floor 50**: +1 Unit Slot & Rare Items (High)
- **Floor 100**: +1 Unit Slot & Secret Unit (Critical)

<Callout type="info" title="Raid Entry Requirements">
Before attempting Raids, ensure your team meets the following criteria:
- At least 3-4 Mythic units equipped.
- All unit slots from Tower Floor 25 unlocked.
- Main units at Level 50 or higher.
- Ability VFX disabled in settings to reduce lag.
</Callout>

| Mode | Primary Reward | Use Case |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Tower** | Unit Slots | Increases total team damage output |
| **Raids** | Stat Cubes | Permanent account-wide talent upgrades |
| **Dungeons** | Rare Loot | Specific evolution materials and gems |
| **Events** | Exclusive Units | Limited-time power spikes for your roster |

</Section>

<Section icon={Sparkles} title="Traits, Talents, and Performance">

To reach the pinnacle of performance in Anime Tactical Simulator, you must look beyond simple levels and rarities. Traits and Talents provide the final layer of optimization, allowing you to tailor your units to specific roles like Boss Killing or Wave Clearing. Additionally, managing your game's performance is vital, as the tactical nature of the game requires precise placement which can be hindered by frame drops.

**Talent System:**
Talents are account-wide upgrades funded by **Stat Cubes** earned in Raids. Unlike unit levels, these benefits apply to every hero you deploy, making them the most valuable long-term investment.

**Trait Rerolling:**
Traits are individual unit modifiers. While tempting, you should avoid chasing "Perfect" traits in the early game. Save your **Premium Rerolls** for Mythic and Secret units that you plan to keep for the remainder of the 2026 season.

<Callout type="tip" title="Lag Reduction">
If you experience FPS drops during intense raids, go to the in-game settings and toggle off **Ability VFX** and **Raid Effects**. This significantly improves stability without affecting your tactical capabilities.
</Callout>

| Performance Setting | Impact | Recommendation |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Ability VFX** | High FPS Boost | Turn OFF for Raids |
| **Raid Effects** | High FPS Boost | Turn OFF for Raids |
| **Graphics Quality** | Medium FPS Boost | Set to Manual (Level 1-3) |
| **View Distance** | Low FPS Boost | Reduce in large maps |

</Section>

<Section icon={Shield} title="Frequently Asked Questions">

<FAQ items={[
  {
    question: "What is the best way to get Gems in Anime Tactical Simulator?",
    answer: "The most efficient way to get Gems is by redeeming all active codes from the anime tactical simulator wiki, completing daily missions, and progressing through the Tower floors, which offer milestone rewards."
  },
  {
    question: "How do I unlock more unit slots?",
    answer: "Unit slots are primarily unlocked through Tower Mode. Reaching floors 10, 25, 50, and 100 provides permanent additional slots, which are essential for increasing your total team damage."
  },
  {
    question: "When should I start evolving my units?",
    answer: "Evolution should begin in World 3. In the early game, focus on building a diverse roster of Mythics. Once you have a stable team and excess Souls, start evolving your primary damage dealers."
  },
  {
    question: "Why aren't my codes working?",
    answer: "Codes are case-sensitive and often require you to join the official Tsukikage Roblox group. Ensure you are typing the code exactly as shown and check if it has expired."
  }
]} />

</Section>
